title: Gulls In An Aery Morrice
content:
Gulls in an aery morrice
    Gleam and vanish and gleam . . .
    The full sea, sleepily basking,
    Dreams under skies of dream.

    Gulls in an aery morrice
    Circle and swoop and close . . .
    Fuller and ever fuller
    The rose of the morning blows.

    Gulls, in an aery morrice
    Frolicking, float and fade . . .
    O, the way of a bird in the sunshine,
    The way of a man with a maid!
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Ernest Henley, titled "Gulls In An Aery Morrice", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
